channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Scam Compounds in Myanmar Expand Despite Crackdowns, Satellite Images Show
item.description: Satellite imagery analysis reveals that over 25 suspected scam compounds have emerged or expanded significantly in Myanmar's Myawaddy region near the Thai border since early 2026. These operations, of...

item.title: News Corp Australia Blocks Crawler Bots with Access Restrictions
item.description: The article explains that News Corp Australia has implemented software to manage crawler bot traffic on its websites. Users who are blocked as crawler bots and are not actual readers or subscribers ar...

item.title: Economic Impact of Migration Restrictions: Lessons from Brexit and South Africa's Immigration Reforms
item.description: Standard Bank Group CEO Sim Tshabalala has warned that South Africa should heed the UK's Brexit experience, highlighting the economic costs of migration restrictions. Speaking at the Kgalema Motlanthe...

item.title: Wildfires force mass evacuations in south-west France as emergency services also battle major blaze near Madrid
item.description: Large wildfires in south-west France have forced the evacuation of tens of thousands of residents and holidaymakers from the Cap Ferret peninsula and nearby Biscarrosse, while Spain is also dealing wi...

item.title: ICE Violates Court Orders by Deporting Man to Two Countries
item.description: The Trump administration's Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has violated two separate court orders by deporting Nelson Ariel Umanzor Chavez to Mexico and El Salvador, both in illegal actions....

item.title: Arusha RC and Japan's Yoichi Discuss Strengthening Bilateral Ties Ahead of IPU Assembly
item.description: The article highlights a meeting between Arusha Regional Council (RC) and Japan's Yoichi, focusing on strengthening bilateral relations as Yoichi's region prepares for the International Parliamentary ...

item.title: Digital Services and Network Expansion Boost Africa's Telecom Growth
item.description: The article highlights how digital services and network expansion are driving significant growth in Africa's telecommunications sector. It mentions initiatives by various countries to improve connecti...

item.title: NFL Player Isaiah Adams Faces Firearm Charges in Durham Investigation
item.description: Isaiah Adams, a 25-year-old offensive lineman for the Arizona Cardinals, is facing multiple firearm-related charges following a Durham Regional Police investigation into drug trafficking and illegal w...

item.title: Government aims to boost competition in power distribution to enhance service quality
item.description: The Indian government is introducing a reform to allow multiple electricity suppliers to use the same distribution network, aiming to improve service quality, reduce infrastructure duplication, and gi...

item.title: Osun Govt Clarifies NURTW Suspension, Reaffirms Leadership
item.description: The Osun State Government has officially denied claims that Governor Ademola Adeleke dissolved the leadership of the National Union of Road Transport Workers (NURTW). In a statement released by the Co...

item.title: President Marcos inaugurates Pampanga children's hospital, pledges expansion of specialty care
item.description: President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. inaugurated the Jose B. Lingad Children’s Medical Center in Mabalacat, Pampanga, marking the first facility under the Clark Multi-Specialty Medical Center project. Th...

item.title: LeBron James commits to Philadelphia 76ers for two-year contract
item.description: NBA legend LeBron James has finalized his decision to join the Philadelphia 76ers on a two-year, $8 million deal. The 41-year-old superstar, who had kept the basketball world guessing after leaving th...

item.title: Aston Martin Hopes Hungary Upgrade Revives Team's Performance
item.description: Adrian Newey, the top Formula One designer and team principal for Aston Martin, expressed optimism after the team's heavily upgraded car made its track debut at the Hungarian Grand Prix. Despite strug...

item.title: Global markets fluctuate as oil prices drop and AI stocks face sell-off
item.description: Global stock markets showed mixed performance on July 24, 2026, with oil prices declining for the first time in a week. Asian markets experienced significant declines amid a widespread sell-off of art...

item.title: Nurses Urge Probe Into Hospital Director's Handling Of Social Media Disciplinary Case
item.description: The Elegant Nurses Forum (ENF) has condemned the disciplinary action taken against a nurse at Kaduna Federal Neuro-Psychiatric Hospital over social media comments, calling it an abuse of power and vio...

item.title: Understanding the Indian Election Process: A Guide for Voters
item.description: The article explains the step-by-step process for voting in India during the 2024 general elections. It highlights the importance of voter registration, the role of the Election Commission, and the pr...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Results: One Winner Claims Over P20M Megalotto Jackpot on July 24, 2026
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced on July 24, 2026, that there was one winner of the over P20 million Megalotto 6/45 jackpot draw. The winning combination of 14-20-19-31-02-11...

item.title: Vice President Nchimbi emphasizes justice as foundation for peace and national unity
item.description: Vice President Emmanuel Nchimbi has emphasized that justice is essential for achieving lasting peace and national unity in Tanzania. Speaking at the Tanzania Human Rights Defenders Coalition (THRDC) G...

item.title: Meghan Markle's family visits Princess Diana's childhood home during summer holidays
item.description: Meghan Markle has shared photographs of her family's summer holiday activities, including a visit to the childhood home of Princess Diana. The Duchess of Sussex, along with Prince Harry and their chil...

item.title: Defense Secretary Hegseth faces impeachment over war powers allegations
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth is under investigation for potential impeachment over allegations of violating the War Powers Resolution. Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) intensified the case by citin...
